response.justification: While Bogdan is an accomplished software engineer with strong backend and systems development experience, the Principal Performance Architect role requires deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, and deep learning workload optimization. His background is primarily in web/backend services and software infrastructure, with only tangential hardware exposure (C/C++ at National Instruments, D lang systems work). The specialized nature of hardware-software co-design for AI accelerators, SIMD/MIMD architecture, and performance modeling represents a significant skills gap.
strengths: Demonstrated performance optimization skills through low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby with caching strategies and high-performance databases handling millions of requests
Broad technical versatility with experience across multiple languages and system levels (C/C++, D lang, backend/infrastructure) showing adaptability
Current exposure to AI infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq demonstrates awareness of contemporary AI systems and infrastructure challenges
concerns: No evidence of SoC or IP modeling expertise—this core requirement demands specialized hardware design knowledge distinct from software engineering experience
Lacks deep learning and neural network optimization background; performance optimization experience is for web services and data pipelines, not AI accelerators
Career trajectory is predominantly software-centric with minimal hardware microarchitecture design work required for principal-level hardware architecture role

key_factors.factor: Software infrastructure vs. hardware architecture focus
